title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure poses a significant threat to organizations relying on cloud environments. By exploiting compromised CI/CD pipelines, attackers can deploy malicious code, potentially leading to data encryption, service disruptions, and financial losses.
recommended_actions: Conduct an immediate review of CI/CD pipelines to identify and remediate any unauthorized access or misconfigurations.
Implement robust monitoring and anomaly detection tools to identify unusual activity within cloud environments.
Ensure regular backups of critical data are performed and stored securely offline to mitigate the impact of ransomware attacks.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

block: fix zones_cond memory...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

block: fix zones_cond memory leak on zone revalidation error paths

When blk_revalidate_disk_zones() fails after disk_revalidate_zone_resources()
has allocated args.zones_cond, the memory is leaked because no error path
frees it.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-53127.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

fs/omfs: reject...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

fs/omfs: reject s_sys_blocksize smaller than OMFS_DIR_START

omfs_fill_super() rejects oversized s_sys_blocksize values (> PAGE_SIZE),
but it does not reject values smaller than OMFS_DIR_START (0x1b8 = 440).

Later, omfs_make_empty() uses

    sbi->s_sys_blocksize - OMFS_DIR_START

as the length argument to memset().  Since s_sys_blocksize is u32,
a crafted filesystem image with s_sys_blocksize < OMFS_DIR_START causes
an unsign

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-53130.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

vdpa: use generic...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

vdpa: use generic driver_override infrastructure

When a driver is probed through __driver_attach(), the bus' match()
callback is called without the device lock held, thus accessing the
driver_override field without a lock, which can cause a UAF.

Fix this by using the driver-core driver_override infrastructure taking
care of proper locking internally.

Note that calling match() from __driver_attach() without the device lock
he

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the Linux kernel's vdpa subsystem could lead to a Use-After-Free (UAF) condition, potentially allowing attackers to exploit the system for privilege escalation, denial of service, or arbitrary code execution. This poses a significant risk to systems relying on the affected kernel versions.
recommended_actions: Immediately update to the latest patched version of the Linux kernel that resolves CVE-2026-53118.
Review and monitor systems for unusual activity or signs of exploitation, especially in environments using the vdpa subsystem.
Implement additional monitoring and logging to detect potential exploitation attempts targeting this vulnerability.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

md: fix array_state=clear...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

md: fix array_state=clear sysfs deadlock

When "clear" is written to array_state, md_attr_store() breaks sysfs
active protection so the array can delete itself from its own sysfs
store method.

However, md_attr_store() currently drops the mddev reference before
calling sysfs_unbreak_active_protection(). Once do_md_stop(..., 0)
has made the mddev eligible for delayed deletion, the temporary
kobject reference taken by sysfs_break

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-53125.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

blk-cgroup: fix disk...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

blk-cgroup: fix disk reference leak in blkcg_maybe_throttle_current()

Add the missing put_disk() on the error path in
blkcg_maybe_throttle_current(). When blkcg lookup, blkg lookup, or
blkg_tryget() fails, the function jumps to the out label which only
calls rcu_read_unlock() but does not release the disk reference acquired
by blkcg_schedule_throttle() via get_device(). Since current->throttle_disk
is already set to NULL befor

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the Linux kernel could lead to resource leaks, such as disk reference leaks, which may degrade system performance over time or cause system instability. This could be exploited in specific scenarios to disrupt services or cause denial of service (DoS).
recommended_actions: Immediately update the Linux kernel to the latest version that includes the patch for CVE-2026-53126.
Monitor systems for unusual disk activity or performance degradation that may indicate exploitation attempts.
Conduct a thorough review of systems running affected Linux kernel versions and prioritize patching critical infrastructure.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

drbd: Balance RCU calls in...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

drbd: Balance RCU calls in drbd_adm_dump_devices()

Make drbd_adm_dump_devices() call rcu_read_lock() before
rcu_read_unlock() is called. This has been detected by the Clang
thread-safety analyzer.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-53128.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

amd-pstate: Fix memory leak...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

amd-pstate: Fix memory leak in amd_pstate_epp_cpu_init()

On failure to set the epp, the function amd_pstate_epp_cpu_init()
returns with an error code without freeing the cpudata object that was
allocated at the beginning of the function.

Ensure that the cpudata object is freed before returning from the
function.

This memory leak was discovered by Claude Opus 4.6 with the aid of
Chris Mason's AI review-prompts
(https://github

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-53121.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

fs/mbcache: cancel shrink...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

fs/mbcache: cancel shrink work before destroying the cache

mb_cache_destroy() calls shrinker_free() and then frees all cache
entries and the cache itself, but it does not cancel the pending
c_shrink_work work item first.

If mb_cache_entry_create() schedules c_shrink_work via schedule_work()
and the work item is still pending or running when mb_cache_destroy()
runs, mb_cache_shrink_worker() will access the cache after its memo

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the Linux kernel's mbcache subsystem could lead to a use-after-free condition if a scheduled shrink work item accesses the cache after it has been destroyed. This could potentially allow attackers to exploit the system, leading to crashes, data corruption, or arbitrary code execution.
recommended_actions: Ensure that all Linux systems are updated to the latest kernel version where this vulnerability has been patched.
Monitor system logs and kernel behavior for any unusual activity that may indicate exploitation attempts.
Implement runtime protections and memory safety mechanisms to mitigate potential use-after-free vulnerabilities.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

ublk: reset per-IO canceled...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

ublk: reset per-IO canceled flag on each fetch

If a ublk server starts recovering devices but dies before issuing fetch
commands for all IOs, cancellation of the fetch commands that were
successfully issued may never complete. This is because the per-IO
canceled flag can remain set even after the fetch for that IO has been
submitted - the per-IO canceled flags for all IOs in a queue are reset
together only once all IOs for tha

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-53124.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

PCI: use generic...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

PCI: use generic driver_override infrastructure

When a driver is probed through __driver_attach(), the bus' match()
callback is called without the device lock held, thus accessing the
driver_override field without a lock, which can cause a UAF.

Fix this by using the driver-core driver_override infrastructure taking
care of proper locking internally.

Note that calling match() from __driver_attach() without the device lock
hel

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the Linux kernel's PCI driver handling could lead to a Use-After-Free (UAF) condition, potentially allowing attackers to execute arbitrary code, cause system crashes, or escalate privileges. This poses a significant risk to systems running affected Linux kernel versions.
recommended_actions: Immediately update to the latest Linux kernel version that includes the patch for CVE-2026-53120.
Review and monitor systems for any signs of exploitation or unusual activity, particularly in environments where PCI devices are used.
Implement strict access controls and network segmentation to limit the potential impact of exploitation.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

btrfs: fix deadlock between...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

btrfs: fix deadlock between reflink and transaction commit when using flushoncommit

When using the flushoncommit mount option, we can have a deadlock between
a transaction commit and a reflink operation that copied an inline extent
to an offset beyond the current i_size of the destination node.

The deadlock happens like this:

1) Task A clones an inline extent from inode X to an offset of inode Y
   that is beyond Y's current

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the Linux kernel's Btrfs file system could lead to a system deadlock when using the 'flushoncommit' mount option. This may result in system crashes, data corruption, or unavailability of critical services relying on the affected file system.
recommended_actions: Immediately update to the latest Linux kernel version that includes the patch for CVE-2026-53122.
Audit systems using the Btrfs file system with the 'flushoncommit' mount option and assess the risk of potential deadlocks.
Implement monitoring and alerting mechanisms to detect unusual system behavior or performance degradation in systems using Btrfs.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

md: wake raid456 reshape...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

md: wake raid456 reshape waiters before suspend

During raid456 reshape, direct IO across the reshape position can sleep
in raid5_make_request() waiting for reshape progress while still
holding an active_io reference. If userspace then freezes reshape and
writes md/suspend_lo or md/suspend_hi, mddev_suspend() kills active_io
and waits for all in-flight IO to drain.

This can deadlock: the IO needs reshape progress to continue,

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-53123.

driver_override field without a lock, which can cause a UAF.

Fix this by using the driver-core driver_override infrastructure taking
care of proper locking internally.

Note that calling match() from __driver_attach() without the device

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-53119.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

bpf: Switch CONFIG_CFI_CLANG...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

bpf: Switch CONFIG_CFI_CLANG to CONFIG_CFI

This was renamed in commit 23ef9d439769 ("kcfi: Rename CONFIG_CFI_CLANG
to CONFIG_CFI") as it is now a compiler-agnostic option. Using the wrong
name results in the code getting compiled out. Meaning the CFI failures
for btf_dtor_kfunc_t would still trigger.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability affects the Linux kernel's handling of Control Flow Integrity (CFI) when using the BPF subsystem. If improperly configured, this could lead to potential exploitation by attackers to bypass security mechanisms or cause system instability.
recommended_actions: Ensure all systems running affected Linux kernel versions are updated to include the patch resolving this vulnerability.
Audit and verify kernel configurations to confirm that CONFIG_CFI is properly enabled and configured.
Monitor for any unusual activity or exploitation attempts targeting the BPF subsystem in your environment.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

bpf: Do not allow deleting...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

bpf: Do not allow deleting local storage in NMI

Currently, local storage may deadlock when deferring freeing selem or
local storage through kfree_rcu(), call_rcu() or call_rcu_tasks_trace()
in NMI or reentrant. Since deleting selem in NMI is an unlikely use
case, partially mitigate it by returning error when calling from
bpf_xxx_storage_delete() helpers in NMI. Note that, it is still possible
to deadlock through reentrant. A f

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-53106.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

wifi: mt76: mt7915: fix use...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

wifi: mt76: mt7915: fix use-after-free bugs in mt7915_mac_dump_work()

When the mt7915 pci chip is detaching, the mt7915_crash_data is
released in mt7915_coredump_unregister(). However, the work item
dump_work may still be running or pending, leading to UAF bugs
when the already freed crash_data is dereferenced again in
mt7915_mac_dump_work().

The race condition can occur as follows:

CPU 0 (removal path)               | CPU 1

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-53098.

driver_override field without a lock, which can cause a UAF.

Fix this by using the driver-core driver_override infrastructure taking
care of proper locking internally.

Note that calling match() from __driver_attach() without the device loc

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

wifi: mt76: mt7925: fix...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

wifi: mt76: mt7925: fix potential deadlock in mt7925_roc_abort_sync

roc_abort_sync() can deadlock with roc_work(). roc_work() holds
dev->mt76.mutex, while cancel_work_sync() waits for roc_work()
to finish. If the caller already owns the same mutex, both
sides block and no progress is possible.

This deadlock can occur during station removal when
mt76_sta_state() -> mt76_sta_remove() ->
mt7925_mac_sta_remove_link() -> mt7925_ma

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability involves a potential deadlock in the Linux kernel's mt76 WiFi driver, specifically affecting the mt7925 chipset. This could lead to system instability or denial of service (DoS) in scenarios where the deadlock condition is triggered, particularly during station removal processes.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest Linux kernel patch that resolves CVE-2026-53103 to affected systems.
Audit systems using the mt76 WiFi driver to identify potential exposure to this vulnerability.
Monitor vendor advisories and security bulletins for further updates or mitigations.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

wifi: mt76: mt7921: fix...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

wifi: mt76: mt7921: fix potential deadlock in mt7921_roc_abort_sync

roc_abort_sync() can deadlock with roc_work(). roc_work() holds
dev->mt76.mutex, while cancel_work_sync() waits for roc_work()
to finish. If the caller already owns the same mutex, both
sides block and no progress is possible.

This deadlock can occur during station removal when
mt76_sta_state() -> mt76_sta_remove() -> mt7921_mac_sta_remove() ->
mt7921_roc_abo

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-53101.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

wifi: mt76: Fix memory leak...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

wifi: mt76: Fix memory leak destroying device

All MT76 rx queues have an associated page_pool even if the queue is not
associated to a NAPI (e.g. WED RRO queues with WED enabled). Destroy the
page_pool running mt76_dma_cleanup routine during module unload.
Moreover returns pages to the page pool if WED is not enabled for WED RRO
queues.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the Linux kernel's MT76 WiFi driver could lead to a memory leak, potentially causing system instability or resource exhaustion. This could be exploited in specific scenarios, particularly in environments relying on MT76-based WiFi devices.
recommended_actions: Ensure the Linux kernel is updated to the latest version that includes the patch for CVE-2026-53104.
Audit systems using MT76-based WiFi devices to identify potential exposure and apply mitigations if updates are not immediately feasible.
Monitor system performance for signs of resource exhaustion or instability, particularly in environments using MT76 WiFi drivers.

title: CVE-2026-35275 - Vulnerability in the Oracle VM VirtualBox product of Oracle Virtualization (component: Shared Folder
description: Vulnerability in the Oracle VM VirtualBox product of Oracle Virtualization (component: Shared Folders).   The supported version that is affected is 7.2.8. Difficult to exploit vulnerability allows low privileged attacker with logon to the infrastructure where Oracle VM VirtualBox executes to compromise Oracle VM VirtualBox.  While the vulnerability is in Oracle VM VirtualBox, attacks may significantly impact additional products (scope change).  Successful attacks of this vulnerability can result in  unauthorized creation, deletion or modification access to critical data or all Oracle VM VirtualBox accessible data as well as  unauthorized access to critical data or complete access to all Oracle VM VirtualBox accessible data. CVSS 3.1 Base Score 7.5 (Confidentiality and Integrity impacts).  CVSS Vector: (CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:H/PR:L/UI:N/S:C/C:H/I:H/A:N).

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability in Oracle VM VirtualBox's Shared Folders component allows a low-privileged attacker with access to the infrastructure to compromise the VirtualBox environment. The scope change indicates that successful exploitation could extend to other connected systems or products, potentially leading to data breaches, privilege escalation, or disruption of services.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply the latest security patches or updates provided by Oracle for VirtualBox version 7.2.8.
Restrict access to the VirtualBox infrastructure by limiting user permissions and ensuring only trusted users have access.
Monitor systems for unusual activity or signs of exploitation, and implement network segmentation to limit the impact of a potential breach.

title: CVE-2026-35274 - Vulnerability in the PeopleSoft Enterprise PT PeopleTools product of Oracle PeopleSoft (component: D
description: Vulnerability in the PeopleSoft Enterprise PT PeopleTools product of Oracle PeopleSoft (component: Deployment Package).  Supported versions that are affected are 8.61 and  8.62. Easily exploitable vulnerability allows unauthenticated attacker with network access via HTTP to compromise PeopleSoft Enterprise PT PeopleTools.  Successful attacks of this vulnerability can result in  unauthorized access to critical data or complete access to all PeopleSoft Enterprise PT PeopleTools accessible data as well as  unauthorized update, insert or delete access to some of PeopleSoft Enterprise PT PeopleTools accessible data. CVSS 3.1 Base Score 8.2 (Confidentiality and Integrity impacts).  CVSS Vector: (CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:L/A:N).

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-35274.

title: CVE-2026-35259 - Vulnerability in the WebLogic Server product of Oracle Fusion Middleware (component: Console).  Supp
description: Vulnerability in the WebLogic Server product of Oracle Fusion Middleware (component: Console).  Supported versions that are affected are 14.1.2.0.0 and  15.1.1.0.0. Easily exploitable vulnerability allows unauthenticated attacker with network access via HTTPS to compromise WebLogic Server.  Successful attacks require human interaction from a person other than the attacker. Successful attacks of this vulnerability can result in takeover of WebLogic Server. CVSS 3.1 Base Score 8.8 (Confidentiality, Integrity and Availability impacts).  CVSS Vector: (CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:R/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H).

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-35259.

title: CVE-2026-35258 - Vulnerability in the WebLogic Server product of Oracle Fusion Middleware (component: Console).  Supp
description: Vulnerability in the WebLogic Server product of Oracle Fusion Middleware (component: Console).  Supported versions that are affected are 14.1.2.0.0 and  15.1.1.0.0. Easily exploitable vulnerability allows low privileged attacker with network access via HTTPS to compromise WebLogic Server.  Successful attacks require human interaction from a person other than the attacker and while the vulnerability is in WebLogic Server, attacks may significantly impact additional products (scope change). Successful attacks of this vulnerability can result in  unauthorized creation, deletion or modification access to critical data or all WebLogic Server accessible data as well as  unauthorized access to critical data or complete access to all WebLogic Server accessible data. CVSS 3.1 Base Score 8.7 (Confidentiality and Integrity impacts).  CVSS Vector: (CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:R/S:C/C:H/I:H/A:N).

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-35258.

title: CVE-2026-12348 - Address bar spoofing in Arc Search for Android allows a remote attacker to display a trusted domain
description: Address bar spoofing in Arc Search for Android allows a remote attacker to display a trusted domain in the address bar while rendering attacker-controlled content, enabling phishing.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-12348.
